message:
Replace gconv dlsym-forwarding stubs with native safe-defaults

The GLIBC_PRIVATE gconv functions (__gconv_create_spec, __gconv_destroy_spec,
__gconv_get_alias_db, __gconv_get_cache, __gconv_get_modules_db, __gconv,
__gconv_transliterate) previously used dlsym(RTLD_NEXT) to forward calls
to the host glibc. This is fragile: it silently fails when no host glibc
is present (static linking, musl, cross-compilation) and drags in the
entire gconv step-chain machinery that FrankenLibC does not use.

Replace each stub with a self-contained safe-default:
- create_spec: zero-initialise the caller's spec buffer (inert)
- destroy_spec: no-op (nothing was allocated)
- get_alias_db / get_cache / get_modules_db: return null
- __gconv: set *written = 0, return GCONV_NOCONV
- __gconv_transliterate: return GCONV_NOCONV

These are correct because FrankenLibC's public iconv surface
(iconv_open/iconv/iconv_close) handles encoding conversion directly
without the gconv step chain. Returning NOCONV / null signals
"unavailable" to any code that probes the private API, which is the
same behaviour as the old dlsym path when the symbol was missing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
